# Ratcheting conformance skip-list. See tests/Celly.Conformance/KnownFailures.cs.
#
# Entries: exact "file/section/test" ids, "prefix/*" globs, or "*" for everything.
# A listed case that fails is an expected failure; a listed case that PASSES fails
# the build until its entry is removed — the pass rate can only go up.
#
# Regenerate: CELLY_CONFORMANCE_REPORT=<file> dotnet test tests/Celly.Conformance,
# then list the FAIL lines here. Status: M6 (2438/2456 = 99.3% passing).
#
# The only remaining entries are the strong-enum mode sections: they re-run the
# same expressions as the (passing) legacy enum sections with mutually exclusive
# expected results, so no single-configuration implementation can pass both.
# cel-go's conformance runner skips them as well.
enums/strong_proto2/assign_standalone_int
enums/strong_proto2/convert_int_big
enums/strong_proto2/convert_int_inrange
enums/strong_proto2/convert_int_neg
enums/strong_proto2/convert_string
enums/strong_proto2/field_type
enums/strong_proto2/type_global
enums/strong_proto2/type_nested
enums/strong_proto3/assign_standalone_int
enums/strong_proto3/assign_standalone_int_big
enums/strong_proto3/assign_standalone_int_neg
enums/strong_proto3/convert_int_big
enums/strong_proto3/convert_int_inrange
enums/strong_proto3/convert_int_neg
enums/strong_proto3/convert_string
enums/strong_proto3/field_type
enums/strong_proto3/type_global
enums/strong_proto3/type_nested
